languageExamples.description: In the evolution from Latin to French, the Latin /j/ or soft /g/ developed into [dʒ], which in some positions later simplified. While the main path was to [ʒ], some dialectal variations show further reduction to [z].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
preamble: De-affrication and de-palatalization of the voiced palato-alveolar affricate [dʒ] to the voiced alveolar fricative [z]. This process involves both the loss of the stop component (de-affrication) and the forward shift in articulation (de-palatalization).
